strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js and React experience across multiple production roles (Optilyz, RIDE, re:cinq), directly matching the core required stack
Hands-on LLM/AI-agent and MCP integration work plus Docker/Kubernetes and AWS experience, covering nearly every nice-to-have qualification
Strong clean-architecture and TDD craftsmanship background, with mission alignment via years building the GISCollective green-mapping platform for environmental NGOs
concerns: Significantly more senior than the entry-level profile, which may raise compensation and long-term retention questions
Relational database experience lists MySQL/SQL and MongoDB but not PostgreSQL specifically, and Nest.js is not explicitly mentioned
Current career gap since October 2025, though recent re:cinq work keeps skills fresh

key_factors.factor: Exact stack match (TypeScript, React, Node, REST)
LLM, ML and cloud/Kubernetes nice-to-haves already covered
Berlin-based, compatible with high on-site presence

response.justification: This candidate offers an unusually strong fit for the role's technical requirements: a decade-plus of fullstack work centered on TypeScript, Node.js and React, proven REST and web-architecture depth, and recent AI-agent/LLM integration experience that covers the posting's nice-to-haves outright. His long-running open-source GISCollective platform — mapping green spaces for NGOs, deployable on Kubernetes — signals genuine affinity for the renewable-energy mission, not just the tech. Based in Berlin and demonstrably collaborative (workshops, mentoring, pairing, TDD culture), he could contribute from day one across database to UI. The main question is seniority alignment rather than capability, and his explicit craftsman ethos and history of taking progressively broader ownership argue he would elevate the team rather than outgrow it.
